axiom StrongPerimeter : Prop
axiom ManyTurnovers : Prop
axiom PaintProtected : Prop
axiom LimitInside : Prop

-- If the team plays strong perimeter defense, then they will force many turnovers.
axiom R1 : StrongPerimeter → ManyTurnovers
-- If the big men protect the paint, then they will limit inside scoring.
axiom R2 : PaintProtected → LimitInside
-- Either the perimeter defense will be strong or the paint will be protected (or both).
axiom R3 : StrongPerimeter ∨ PaintProtected

-- From the premises, we can derive: If they did not limit inside scoring, then they forced many turnovers.
theorem turnovers_if_not_limited : ¬LimitInside → ManyTurnovers := by
  intro hNotL
  -- From ¬LimitInside and (PaintProtected → LimitInside), deduce ¬PaintProtected
  have hNotB : ¬PaintProtected := by
    intro hB
    exact hNotL (R2 hB)
  -- From StrongPerimeter ∨ PaintProtected and ¬PaintProtected, deduce StrongPerimeter
  have hP : StrongPerimeter := by
    cases R3 with
    | inl hP => exact hP
    | inr hB =>
      have : False := hNotB hB
      exact False.elim this
  -- Then force many turnovers
  exact R1 hP
</lean>

<lean>
axiom ScoreMoreA : Prop
axiom WinA : Prop
axiom FumblesB : Prop
axiom LoseB : Prop

-- If Team A scores more, they win.
axiom H1 : ScoreMoreA → WinA
-- If Team B fumbles a lot, they lose.
axiom H2 : FumblesB → LoseB
-- Either A scores more, or B fumbles a lot (or both).
axiom H3 : ScoreMoreA ∨ FumblesB

-- From the premises, the queried implication actually holds.
theorem implication_holds : (¬LoseB) → ScoreMoreA := by
  intro hNotLose
  cases H3 with
  | inl hA => exact hA
  | inr hFB =>
    have hLose : LoseB := H2 hFB
    have : False := hNotLose hLose
    exact False.elim this

-- Consequently, there cannot be a counterexample (¬LoseB ∧ ¬ScoreMoreA).
theorem no_counterexample : ¬(¬LoseB ∧ ¬ScoreMoreA) := by
  intro h
  have hNotLose : ¬LoseB := And.left h
  have hNotA : ¬ScoreMoreA := And.right h
  have hA : ScoreMoreA := implication_holds hNotLose
  exact hNotA hA
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

  exact nh h

  have d := Disj

    | inr both => exact Or.inl both.left

  intro nL
  have d : CloseToLunch ∨ ¬Ready John := L_or_notR
  cases d with

  intro d
  have pw : PreparedWell John := DtoP John d
  exact PtoR John pw

-- From ¬R derive ¬D
theorem notR_to_notD : ¬Ready John → ¬Diligent John := by
  intro nR
  intro d
  have r : Ready John := D_implies_R d
  exact nR r

-- From ¬Hungry derive ¬Diligent
theorem notH_to_notD : ¬CoworkersHungry John → ¬Diligent John := by
  intro nh
  have nL : ¬CloseToLunch := notH_to_notL nh
  have nR : ¬Ready John := notL_to_notR nL
  exact notR_to_notD nR
</lean>

axiom Fit_implies_Active : ∀ x : obj, Fit x → Active x
axiom Energy_implies_Energetic : ∀ x : obj, Energy x → Energetic x

  apply Energy_implies_Energetic Jane
  apply Eat_implies_Energy Jane
  exact Jane_eats_nutritious

  refine ⟨x0, ?_, ?_⟩
  -- prove ¬Exercises x0

  have hFit : Fit x0 := Ex_implies_Fit x0 hxEx
  have hActive : Active x0 := Fit_implies_Active x0 hFit
  exact hNotActive hActive
  -- prove ¬EatsNutritious x0
  intro hxEat
  have hEnergy : Energy x0 := Eat_implies_Energy x0 hxEat
  have hEnergetic : Energetic x0 := Energy_implies_Energetic x0 hEnergy
  exact hNotEnergetic hEnergetic

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MadeFriends x → Enjoy x
-- If someone joins a club, then they will be busy.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, JoinClub x → Busy x
-- Either they made friends, or they are not busy.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, MadeFriends x ∨ ¬Busy x
-- If someone is outgoing, then they would join a club.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Outgoing x → JoinClub x
-- If someone introduced themselves to classmates, then they are outgoing.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Outgoing x

-- From the premises we can derive: if Mark did not enjoy, then Mark did not introduce himself.
theorem not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced : (¬Enjoy Mark) → ¬Introduced Mark := by
  intro hne
  -- From ¬Enjoy Mark and (MadeFriends → Enjoy), we get ¬MadeFriends Mark
  have h_not_mf : ¬MadeFriends Mark := by
    intro mf
    have enj : Enjoy Mark := A1 Mark mf
    exact hne enj
  -- From (MadeFriends ∨ ¬Busy) and ¬MadeFriends, we get ¬Busy
  have disj : MadeFriends Mark ∨ ¬Busy Mark := A3 Mark
  have h_not_busy : ¬Busy Mark := by
    cases disj with
    | inl mf => exact False.elim (h_not_mf mf)
    | inr nb => exact nb
  -- From (JoinClub → Busy) and ¬Busy, we get ¬JoinClub
  have h_not_join : ¬JoinClub Mark := by
    intro hj
    have b : Busy Mark := A2 Mark hj
    exact h_not_busy b
  -- From (Outgoing → JoinClub) and ¬JoinClub, we get ¬Outgoing
  have h_not_out : ¬Outgoing Mark := by
    intro ho
    have hj : JoinClub Mark := A4 Mark ho
    exact h_not_join hj
  -- From (Introduced → Outgoing) and ¬Outgoing, we get ¬Introduced
  intro hi
  have ho : Outgoing Mark := A5 Mark hi
  exact h_not_out ho

  -- ¬WeekendComing from R1 and hnr
  have hnotWeek : ¬WeekendComing := by

  have hNotClear : ¬ClearMind Alex := by

  have hNotFinish : ¬FinishTasks Alex := by

    have hc : ClearMind Alex := R2 Alex hf
    exact hNotClear hc
  -- From Organized → FinishTasks and ¬FinishTasks, deduce ¬Organized (for Alex)
  have hNotOrg : ¬Organized Alex := by
    intro ho
    have hf : FinishTasks Alex := R4 Alex ho
    exact hNotFinish hf
  -- From TodoList → Organized and ¬Organized, deduce ¬TodoList (for Alex)
  intro ht
  have ho : Organized Alex := R5 Alex ht
  exact hNotOrg ho
</lean>
